top.title: US charges Cuba's Raúl Castro with murder over 1996 downing of two planes
top.summary: Cuba's current president says the charges against Castro - including conspiracy to kill US nationals and destruction of aircraft - are a "political manoeuvre".

top.title: Rosenberg: Putin enjoys Xi's Chinese welcome but heads home without pipeline deal
top.summary: Russia and China showed they were shoulder-to-shoulder on the world stage, but it became clear there are limits, says the BBC's Russia Editor.

top.title: DR Congo cancels World Cup training camp over Ebola outbreak
top.summary: The outbreak, caused by a rare Ebola species, is thought to have caused 139 deaths so far.

top.title: Nvidia's record result fails to impress investors
top.summary: The chip giant reported more stellar results but its shares fell after-hours as investors wonder if it can keep up its pace of growth amid greater competition.

top.title: TikTok and YouTube 'not safe enough' for kids, says Ofcom
top.summary: YouTube said it worked with experts to provide appropriate experiences. TikTok said it was disappointed Ofcom had not acknowledged its safety features.

top.title: SpaceX files for stock market debut that could make Elon Musk a trillionaire
top.summary: Musk's rocket-maker and satellite internet provider will trade under the ticker SPCX

title.#text: ‘Fuck you, Bambu’: How one private message could change the face of 3D printing
Meta lays off thousands of employees to offset AI investments
In SpaceX’s IPO, Elon Musk is a risk factor
